channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: EV technology is developing quickly. What's it like to cover such a dynamic industry?
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: How the Iran war is wasting American resources
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Former FBI Director and special counsel Robert Mueller has died at 81
item.description: NPR's justice correspondent Carrie Johnson speaks with Adrian Ma about Robert Mueller. The former FBI Director and special counsel has died at the age of 81, his family said.
item.content:encoded: NPR's justice correspondent Carrie Johnson speaks with Adrian Ma about Robert Mueller. The former FBI Director and special counsel has died at the age of 81, his family said.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Need some uplift? Life Kit has tips on how to be more optimistic
item.description: Studies have shown optimism promotes longevity, can lead to higher career success and even reduce risk of chronic disease. Life Kit offers tips on how to be more optimistic.
item.content:encoded: Studies have shown optimism promotes longevity, can lead to higher career success and even reduce risk of chronic disease. Life Kit offers tips on how to be more optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: As the war in Iran expands, can diplomacy help end it?
item.description: After weeks of escalation, can diplomacy end the conflict between Iran, the US and Israel? NPR's Adrian Ma poses that question to former Iranian diplomat Hossein Mousavian.
item.content:encoded: After weeks of escalation, can diplomacy end the conflict between Iran, the US and Israel? NPR's Adrian Ma poses that question to former Iranian diplomat Hossein Mousavian.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: How the U.S. oil blockade is taking a high toll on everyday Cubans
item.description: As tensions between Washington and Havana mount, what is life like for Cubans living through a weekslong oil blockade? NPR's Adrian Ma speaks to CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about life on the island.
item.content:encoded: As tensions between Washington and Havana mount, what is life like for Cubans living through a weekslong oil blockade? NPR's Adrian Ma speaks to CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about life on the island.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Trio Libre: Colombian musician Samuel Torres' new album features just three instruments
item.description: Colombian-born percussionist and composer Samuel Torres and his group release their newest album, Trio Libre.
item.content:encoded: Colombian-born percussionist and composer Samuel Torres and his group release their newest album, Trio Libre.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Iraqi Kurds mark Nowruz, celebrating light over darkness
item.description: In the Kurdish regions of the Middle East, Nowruz celebrations — honoring the arrival of spring — are a fundamental expression of Kurdish identity.
item.content:encoded:
In the Kurdish regions of the Middle East, Nowruz celebrations — honoring the arrival of spring — are a fundamental expression of Kurdish identity.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Liam Conejo Ramos' family fights immigration judge's ruling to deport them to Ecuador
item.description: The family of a 5-year-old Minnesota boy and his father who were detained by U.S. immigration agents in January is now fighting an immigration judge's ruling that rejects their claim for asylum.
item.content:encoded: The family of a 5-year-old Minnesota boy and his father who were detained by U.S. immigration agents in January is now fighting an immigration judge's ruling that rejects their claim for asylum.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina

item.title: Why a small town in Tennessee is at the center of a renewed global arms race
item.description: A small Tennessee town hopes to stop the construction of a facility that has a federal contract to refine depleted uranium into a metallic form the government needs to build nuclear weapons.
item.content:encoded: A small Tennessee town hopes to stop the construction of a facility that has a federal contract to refine depleted uranium into a metallic form the government needs to build nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Pierce Gentry
